final_idea_spec:
hypothesis: "Sentence embeddings from different models will cluster differently based on syntactic complexity, with simpler sentences forming tighter clusters regardless of semantic content."
intervention_family: "embedding_study"
target_component: "syntactic complexity effects on embedding clustering"
implementation_scope:
  lines_estimate: 180
  dependencies: ["sentence-transformers", "sklearn", "numpy", "matplotlib", "nltk", "textstat"]
  compute: "cpu"
  time_estimate_min: 8
proxy_evaluation:
  metric: "silhouette_score_difference"
  baseline: "random sentence grouping clustering score"
  success_threshold: "0.15 higher silhouette score for syntax-based clustering vs random"
full_evaluation:
  dataset: "custom generated sentences with controlled syntactic complexity"
  n_samples: 150
  n_conditions: 3
expected_failure_modes: ["embedding models may not capture syntactic features distinctly", "complexity metrics may not correlate with embedding distances", "sample sentences may be too similar"]
rollback_plan: "switch to comparing semantic similarity clustering vs syntactic complexity clustering if random baseline fails"
